commit.message:
Detect service stop via D-Bus instead of FIFO monitoring

The AsyncFd/POLLHUP approach in jscreen-write didn't work because
systemd keeps the FIFO reader open until the socket unit deactivates.
Replace it with D-Bus PropertiesChanged monitoring on the service
unit's ActiveState — this fires immediately when systemd reports
the unit as inactive/failed, works for both child and socket cases,
and removes the tokio dependency from jscreen-write.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Update auth callback to redirect to /verify if not yet verified

tune: slow heading curriculum + rate-limit goal expansion

Heading curriculum: 20%→60% of training
  Robot starts facing goal, gradually randomizes over 48k iters (not 16k).
  Learn to walk first, then learn to turn from arbitrary yaw.

Goal expansion: add 200-iter cooldown + tighter threshold 0.30→0.25
  Prevents rapid jump (v12 jumped 1.0→4.8m in 50 iters from stag noise).
  At most one +0.2m expansion per 200 iters.

commit.message:
Replace global X hub with per-component UNLABELED_PIPE_N nodes

Instead of one giant "X" node collecting everything unknown, each
unlabeled connected pipe component now gets its own node with:
- Segment count and total length
- Centroid coordinates
- Explanation text for why it's unlabeled

Also prune orphan unlabeled nodes (0 neighbors after placement) since
those are symbol elements, not pipe runs.  Result: 29 meaningful
unlabeled pipe components instead of one meaningless X hub.

commit.message:
Detect FIFO reader closure in jscreen-write via AsyncFd

jscreen-write was blocking on stdin, so it never noticed when the
service closed the FIFO read end. Now uses tokio with AsyncFd to
monitor the FIFO fd for POLLHUP, exiting immediately when the reader
closes. This lets the child_exit select branch in jscreen fire
without waiting for the next keypress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix multi-GB file downloads: move file on disk instead of loading into memory

BackgroundDownloadEngine.didFinishDownloadingTo was reading the entire
downloaded file into a Data object, which fails for large files (e.g.
2.6 GB safetensors). Now moves the file to a stable temp location and
yields a file-based DownloadChunk. DownloadManager detects file-based
chunks and moves them directly to the .part destination.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: CLV snapshot selection bug — was using midnight scrape as opening

MIN(snapshot_tag) returned snap-0006 (midnight scrape) instead of
snap-0700 (morning market open). MAX returned snap-2201 (sparse late)
instead of snap-2200 (evening pre-game close).

Fix: extract numeric time from snapshot_tag, filter to >= 0600 for
opening and <= 2200 for closing.

Impact: 5x more CLV-qualified players (10 vs 2 above 0.5 threshold),
nearly double player coverage (43 vs 24). CLV signals
(positive_clv_over/under) have had ZERO fires for 5 weeks due to
this bug — they should start firing immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rebuild connectivity engine with geometric line tracing

- Fix critical seg.pos vs UF local-index mismatch that corrupted all
  adjacency/label lookups
- Two-pass label assignment: tight direct proximity (0.014×size) + UF
  component propagation; prevents label bleed between parallel pipes
- Add collinearity check (200 UOR off-axis) for same-direction endpoint
  snapping so parallel pipes don't false-connect
- Track shared_endpoints in _build_topology; use directly in
  _detect_terminations to correctly handle crossing-gap connections
- Skip isolated (adj=0) long segments in termination detection — they
  are symbol elements, not open pipe ends
- Create nodes for all 65 classified pipe labels regardless of segment
  assignment so no pipes are silently dropped

Results on M-22EJ01.dgn:
  Named pipe nodes: 65/65 ✓
  Valve nodes:      151/151 ✓
  Terminations:     286 (down from 1200+)
  40-ECB-10" pipe neighbors: 5 (down from 27-34)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(#366): add webhook trigger for tasks

  ANVIL_WEBHOOK_EVENT environment variables
- Dynamic server lifecycle: starts on first subscription, stops when last removed
- Graceful shutdown integration
- StopSubscription support for handler removal
- 20 comprehensive tests covering all acceptance criteria

commit.message:
Add confidence-gated soft chaining + MLP epoch logging

- _run_soft_chained_sklearn: add confidence_threshold param; rows whose
  max predicted probability falls below the threshold receive a uniform
  conditioning block for the next step (signalling "unknown") rather than
  propagating a low-confidence guess; reconstruction of the current
  feature still uses the model's best-guess label
- MLP soft chaining: replace silent clf.fit() with partial_fit loop that
  prints loss every 20 epochs, matching hard-chain MLP verbosity
- run_chaining_analysis.py: add CONFIDENCE_GATED_CHAINING / CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD
  config toggles; wire soft-gated variant through all accumulators, loops,
  tables, and CSV outputs

commit.message:
add serial master lock + update CLAUDE.md with current status

app.py: Added asyncio.Lock around _run_master_reasoning to prevent
concurrent master API calls. Spec requires serial execution — one
reasoning turn at a time. Without the lock, concurrent transcript/tick
events could race into overlapping master turns.

CLAUDE.md: Updated with full current status — what's built, how to test,
master model config, data files, and what's not built yet.

feat: cross-platform support + Google enterprise light theme + preflight checks

Cross-platform (Windows/macOS/Linux):

- Verify runtime/logs dirs writable

- Check xdg-open on Linux

commit.message:
Sprint 1: Dashboard + Demo Mode — full implementation

- FastAPI backend with JWT auth (admin/manager/viewer roles)
- PostgreSQL schema: 5 tables (raw_messages, parsed_messages, alerts, trips, dashboard_users)
- REST API: /api/auth/login, /api/trips, /api/alerts, /api/stats, /api/config, /api/health
- Demo seed: 50 trips, 6 customers, 13 routes, 10 drivers, realistic Russian logistics data
- Demo simulator: background task updating trips/alerts every 30-90s
- Frontend SPA: login, trips table, alerts feed, trip details, statistics (Chart.js)
- Docker Compose: postgres:15 + app, port 8100
- DB migrations auto-run on startup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

tune: minimal penalties — only punish catastrophic failure, not movement

Principle: per-step movement penalties make standing still the safe choice.
Only keep penalties for true catastrophes (joint damage, falling).

Remove: action_smoothness, base_roll_rate, joint_vel/torque_limits (→0)
Reduce: early_termination -10→-3 (less fear of falling = more exploration)
Keep:   joint_pos_limits -1.0 (hard physical limit)

thigh_acc termination already naturally punishes over-aggression.
Let the robot discover smooth movement through trial and error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
